title: Spectral Politics
abstract: The final chapter, “Spectral Politics,” emphasizes the importance of conversing with ghosts as an ethical imperative. The chapter enters into dialogue with a range of ghosts who appear in the works of Karl Marx, Jacques Derrida, Gayatri Spivak, Gloria Anzaldúa, and Toni Morrison, among others. We read how communist ghosts don’t include ghost women, while queer ghosts riddle heterosexual visions. Ghosts exist as exclusions that tug at the borders of our political imagination and anchor a restless vision in frames of justice. Spectral politics serve as a continuous reminder of responsibility that speaks to the past and future and aims to move toward ethical futures that do not keep reenacting violence.
